(3 comments)

Would you please to add some test cases for:

1) Whether the command "lctl lfsck_start" without "-t" will start all the known LFSCK types by default or not.

2) Whether the command "lctl lfsck_start -t namespace,layout" can be properly parsed or not.

Patch Set 3:

(2 comments)

Patch is refreshed to address some comments, but I don't actually know how to construct good test cases that Fan Yong asked for.

Fan Yong, could you please submit a follow-on patch that contains those test cases.

Patch Set 4:

Andreas: I will submit another patch to fix the remained issues. When the old client talks with new server, it will fail the sanity-scrub test_9/test_11 always. There are two possible solutions for that:

1) fix the old test scripts. (2.5.x, 2.4.x)

2) use new lfsck start options "-a" to indicate to start all known lfsck scanning.

Which one is preferred?

Patch Set 4:

I don't see the "-a" option even on master, so I think it makes sense to just stick with this patch.  It enables all testing if no option is specified, and "-t all" is also available.

I think it makes sense to just fix up the 2.4 and 2.5 lfsck to land this patch.

parents.subject: LU-4629 utils: fix input format specifier error

commit.subject: LU-4887 lfsck: run all LFSCK scan types by default
commit.message:
LU-4887 lfsck: run all LFSCK scan types by default

When running "lctl lfsck_start" run all available scan types by
default (scrub, layout, and namespace currently) if none is given.
If '-t' is given, run only the specified scan types. 'scrub' is
always run.

Rename the lfsck_type constants to use a LFSCK_TYPE_ prefix to be
more descriptive.  Add LFSCK_TYPE_ constants to wirecheck.
Add ltn_ prefix to lfsck_type_name structure.

Print the available types from the lfsck_types_names[] array in
the help message instead of hard-coding them in the help message.

Don't return an error if "lctl lfsck_stop" is used when stopped.

Fix up lfsck_namespace /proc stats to use more descriptive names.

Signed-off-by: Fan Yong <fan.yong@intel.com>
Signed-off-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Change-Id: I106483ade0bf07c2920dd95a263acdbe4e500c1e
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/10278
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: James Nunez <james.a.nunez@intel.com>
